content: For a flowing fluid, a dimensionless combination of velocity (V), length scale (l), and acceleration due to gravity (g) would be
explanation:
The only choice that is dimensionless is Option A.

Check dimensions:

[V] = L T⁻¹

[g] = L T⁻²

[l] = L

So for A:$$\frac{V^2}{g\,l}=\frac{(L/T)^2}{(L/T^2)\,L}=\frac{L^2/T^2}{L^2/T^2}=1\quad\text{(dimensionless)}$$

This ratio is actually the square of the Froude number, $$\mathrm{Fr}^2=\frac{V^2}{g\,l}\,. $$

content:
The drag force, $F_D$ on a sphere due to a fluid flowing past the sphere is a function of viscosity, $\mu$, the mass density, $\rho$, the velocity of flow, $V$ and the diameter of the sphere, $D$.

Pick the relevant (one or more) non-dimensional parameter(s) pertaining to the above process from the following list.
explanation:
$$ F_D=F(D, V, \rho, \mu) $$

Derive that,

$$ \begin{aligned} F_D & =k \cdot D^2 v^2 \rho f\left(\frac{M}{\rho v D}\right) \\ m & =1+4, h=3=5 \\ \text { Number of } \pi \text {-term } & =m-n \\ & =5-3=2 \\ \pi_1 & =D^a v^b \rho^c F_D \end{aligned} $$

Dimension:

$$ \begin{aligned} {\left[\mathrm{M}^{\circ} \mathrm{L}^{\circ} \mathrm{T}^{\circ}\right] } & =[\mathrm{L}]^{\mathrm{a}}\left[\mathrm{LT}^{-1}\right]^{\mathrm{b}}\left[\mathrm{ML}^{-3}\right]^{\mathrm{c}}\left[\mathrm{MLT}^{-2}\right] \\ & =\left[\mathrm{M}^{\mathrm{c}+1} \mathrm{~L}^{\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}-3 \mathrm{c}+1} \mathrm{~T}^{-\mathrm{b}-2}\right] \end{aligned} $$

Compare

$$ \begin{aligned} c+1 & =0, c=-1 \\ b+2 & =0 \quad b=-2 \\ a+b-3+1 & =0 \\ a-2+3+1 & =0 \\ a & =-2 \\ \pi_1 & =D^{-2} v^{-2} \rho^{-1} \cdot F_D \\ or\,\,\,\,\,\,\pi_1 & =\frac{F_D}{D^2 v^2 \rho} \\ \pi_2 & =D^a v^b \rho^c \mu \end{aligned} $$

Dimensions

$$ \begin{aligned} {\left[\mathrm{M}^{\circ} \mathrm{L}^{\circ} \mathrm{T}^{\circ}\right] } & =[\mathrm{L}]^a\left[\mathrm{LT}^{-1}\right]^b\left[\mathrm{ML}^{-3}\right]^c\left[\mathrm{ML}^{-1} \mathrm{~T}^{-1}\right] \\ & =\left[\mathrm{M}^{c+1} \mathrm{~L}^{a+b-3 \mathrm{c}-1} \mathrm{~T}^{-b-1}\right] \end{aligned} $$

Compare

$$ \begin{aligned} c+1 & =0, c=-1 \\ -b-1 & =0 \quad b=-1 \\ a+b-3 c+1 & =0 \\ a-1+3-1 & =0 \\ a & =-1 \\ \pi_2 & =D^{-1} v^{-1} \rho^{-1} \mu \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &or\,\,\,\,\, \pi_2=\frac{\mu}{\rho V D} \\ &\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, \pi_2=D^a V^b \rho^c \mu \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { According to Buckingham's } \pi \text {-theorem }\\ &\begin{aligned} f\left(\pi_1, \pi_2\right) & =0 \\ \pi_1 & =k f\left(\pi_2\right) \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} \frac{F_D}{D^2 v^2 \rho} & =k f\left(\frac{\mu}{\rho v D}\right) \\ F_D & =k \cdot D^2 v^2 \rho f\left(\frac{\mu}{\rho v D}\right) \end{aligned}\\ &\text { Hence, the correct answer are (a) and (c). } \end{aligned} $$
